title: Discovery Service: Verifiable Credential Issuance Module (JWT-VC/JWT-VP per VC DM 2.0 and VC-JOSE-COSE)
description: Implement the credential issuance module in the Discovery Service covering the three core VC types defined in TASK-004: SentinelIdentityCredential, ServiceBindingCredential, and AccessGrantCredential. All credentials are issued as JWT-VCs (VC-JOSE-COSE profile), signed with the Discovery issuer key, include Bitstring Status List references for revocation, and are verified internally before distribution. Only credential metadata is persisted — raw credential ciphertext is optionally stored encrypted.
objectives: Deterministic, schema-validated issuance for all three VC types
Issuer key managed by SecretStorage with kid rotation support
Internal self-check (sign → verify) before any credential is delivered

steps: Step 1 - CredentialIssuerService Class: Create services/discovery/src/discovery/services/credential_issuer.py. Class CredentialIssuerService with methods: issue_sentinel_identity(sentinel: SentinelRecord, session) -> str (JWT), issue_service_binding(sentinel: SentinelRecord, service: ServiceRecord, session) -> str, issue_access_grant(consumer_did: str, producer_service_id: str, env: str, scope: list[str], expires_in: int, session) -> str, reissue_on_rotation(credential_id: UUID, session) -> str. All methods return the signed JWT-VC string.

Step 2 - JWT-VC Structure (VC DM 2.0 + VC-JOSE-COSE): JOSE header: { alg: 'EdDSA', kid: '<key_id>', typ: 'vc+jwt' }. JWT payload claims: iss (Discovery DID), sub (subject DID), nbf (not before as Unix timestamp), exp (expiry as Unix timestamp), jti (UUID v4 — credential ID), iat, vc (Verifiable Credential object). The vc object: { '@context': ['https://www.w3.org/ns/credentials/v2'], type: ['VerifiableCredential', '<specific_type>'], issuer: { id: DISCOVERY_DID }, validFrom: ISO-8601, validUntil: ISO-8601, credentialSubject: { id: subject_DID, ...type-specific_claims }, credentialStatus: { id: '<status_list_url>#<index>', type: 'BitstringStatusListEntry', statusPurpose: 'revocation', statusListIndex: int, statusListCredential: '<status_list_url>' } }.

Step 3 - SentinelIdentityCredential Claims: credentialSubject: { id: sentinel_did, role: 'producer'|'consumer', service_id: str, env: 'dev'|'test'|'prod', entity_id: str (gov entity owning the sentinel), registered_at: ISO-8601 }. exp: max 90 days from issuance. jti: unique per credential.

Step 4 - ServiceBindingCredential Claims: credentialSubject: { id: sentinel_did, authorized_service_id: str, authorized_service_did: str, env: str, binding_type: 'producer-proxy' }. aud: service DID. exp: max 1 year. Issued when a sentinel completes onboarding and is verified as the legitimate proxy for a service.

Step 5 - AccessGrantCredential Claims: credentialSubject: { id: consumer_sentinel_did, target_service_id: str, target_service_did: str, env: str, scope: list[str] (e.g. ['read:invoices', 'submit:tax-return']), granted_by: admin_sub, granted_at: ISO-8601 }. aud: producer service DID. exp: configurable (default 30 days, max 365 days). scope items must be registered in TrustPolicyRegistry (TASK-015) for the service.

Step 6 - Status List Integration: Before issuing each VC: call StatusListService.allocate_index(issuer_did, env, credential_type) → returns { status_list_id, index }. Store this reference in the credential's credentialStatus object. Also persist in credentials table: status_list_id, status_list_index.

Step 7 - Signing: Load issuer private key from SecretStorage using SecretStorage.get_key(role='vc_issuer', env=env). Use python-jose or PyJWT to sign. Validate algorithm matches supported list from TASK-003 settings. Raise IssuerKeyUnavailableError if key cannot be loaded.

Step 8 - Internal Self-Check: After signing, run: (a) parse JWT, (b) verify signature using corresponding public key from SecretStorage, (c) validate schema using Pydantic model, (d) check iss/sub/exp/jti present, (e) check credentialStatus reference is well-formed. If any check fails → raise CredentialIssuanceError (do not return partial credential).

Step 9 - DB Persistence: Insert into credentials table (TASK-022): credential_type, issuer_did, subject_did, audience (if set), env, jti, issued_at, expires_at, status='active', status_list_id, status_list_index, is_latest=true. Do NOT store raw JWT text in DB unless encryption is available. If CREDENTIAL_STORAGE_ENCRYPT=true: encrypt JWT with AES-256-GCM using a key from SecretStorage, store ciphertext in encrypted_payload column.

Step 10 - IssuerRegistry Verification: If VERIFY_ISSUER_ON_CHAIN=true: call IssuerRegistryClient.is_issuer_active(DISCOVERY_DID) before issuing. If issuer is not active on-chain → raise IssuerNotActiveError with detail. This prevents issuance if the Discovery node itself has been disabled in the trust registry.

Step 11 - Max TTL Enforcement: Enforce maximum TTL per credential type in settings: SentinelIdentityCredential: max 90 days, ServiceBindingCredential: max 365 days, AccessGrantCredential: max 365 days (configurable). If requested exp exceeds max TTL → clamp to max and log warning.

Step 12 - Reissuance on Rotation: reissue_on_rotation(credential_id) fetches the existing credential metadata, generates a new VC with same claims but new jti + new exp + updated status reference + new signature. Marks old credential is_latest=false. Optionally revokes old credential status bit. Used when the issuer key rotates or when a sentinel requests credential renewal (TASK-029).

files.services/discovery/src/discovery/schemas: Pydantic models for VC payload validation

api_endpoints.description: Issue a SentinelIdentityCredential for a registered sentinel

error_handling.message: Discovery issuer is not registered as active in IssuerRegistry contract
Could not allocate a status list index for this credential
Issued credential failed internal verification — not delivered

error_handling.condition: Scope item not registered for service
error_handling.message: Scope 'delete:all' is not registered for service 'billing-api'
Maximum TTL for AccessGrantCredential is 365 days

security_constraints: Raw JWT-VC only returned once in the issuance API response — not re-retrievable. Store encrypted in DB if CREDENTIAL_STORAGE_ENCRYPT=true.
issuer private key never exposed outside SecretStorage — only the signing result is used
kid in JOSE header must match a key registered in IssuerRegistry on-chain
Internal self-check is mandatory — no credential leaves the service without passing signature + schema verification
All credential issuance operations write to audit_events: actor, credential_type, subject_did, jti
Scope for AccessGrantCredential validated against an allowlist per service — no freeform scope strings in prod

acceptance_criteria: Discovery issues all three VC types and returned JWT-VCs pass internal schema validation
Signature verification with Discovery public key succeeds for all issued credentials
credentialStatus reference is present and valid in all issued VCs
Issuer key rotation produces credentials with new kid; old credentials with old kid still verifiable
Internal self-check catches and rejects a deliberately corrupted credential payload (unit test)
Credential metadata is persisted in credentials table with correct jti, status, and status_list_index
